races.title: Sparks Mayor
races.notes: Incumbent Ed Lawson faces three challengers amid city fiscal concerns. Nonpartisan; open to all Sparks voters. If no candidate exceeds 50% in the June 9 primary, top two advance to the November general.

races.title: Sparks City Council Ward 2
races.notes: Four candidates including appointed/elected incumbent Dian VanderWell. Nonpartisan; if no candidate exceeds 50% in the primary, top two advance to November.

races.title: Sparks City Council Ward 4
races.notes: Open seat; incumbent Charlene Bybee is term-limited and running for mayor. Four candidates. Nonpartisan; top two advance to November if no majority.

races.title: Washoe County School Board Trustee, District F
races.notes: At-large seat opened by Adam Mayberry leaving to run for Sparks City Council. District F covers Sparks, central Reno, and south Reno east of I-580. Three candidates; top two advance to November. Only competitive WCSD board race in 2026 (incumbents Colleen Westlake and James Phoenix unopposed).

candidates.name: Ed Lawson
candidates.bio: Incumbent Sparks mayor since 2020 (appointed after Ron Smith's death, won 2022 by a narrow margin); Sparks resident since 1989 and former longtime city councilmember.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates: Sparks Mayor
KOLO: Mayoral candidates file amid fiscal concerns
2News: Sparks mayoral forum

budget.stance: Says public-safety costs dominate the budget; favors a 20-year plan for financial stability and Nevada property-tax reform.
budget.quote: If we just take police out of our budget right now, that's $48 million, out of a $121 million budget. We wouldn't have no financial problems.

growth.stance: Wants to expand Sparks eastward toward the Storey County employment center and pursue a federal lands bill to increase developable land, changing the city's tax mix.

taxes.stance: Advocates Nevada property-tax reform to make local government more fiscally sustainable.

candidates.name: Charlene Bybee
candidates.bio: Sparks City Council member (Ward 4) since 2014; lifelong Sparks resident, UNR graduate, and former American Airlines flight attendant. Term-limited on council and running for mayor.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates: Meet Charlene Bybee
KOLO: Mayoral candidates file amid fiscal concerns
Nevada Newsmakers: Bybee cites disagreements with Sparks mayor
2News: Sparks mayoral forum

budget.stance: Calls herself a fiscal conservative; notes Sparks recently closed an ~$18M budget gap with layoffs and cuts, and says she declined a council salary raise to preserve city funds.
budget.quote: As a fiscal conservative, it's my most important job. Every dollar we spend matters to me

growth.stance: Acknowledges growth is straining housing and roads, affecting quality of life; supports a connector road and public-private partnerships.
growth.quote: The infrastructure that it's straining is housing, it's roads, and all of those are quality of life

housing.stance: Supports affordable housing through public-private partnerships and a property-tax reset.

candidates.name: Diana Mackey
candidates.bio: Sparks resident since 1999 with a 40-year human-resources career; a late-entry, largely single-issue candidate motivated by opposition to the Red Hawk Golf Course development.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates: Sparks Mayor
2News: Sparks mayoral forum

growth.stance: Opposes rapid expansion, especially the Red Hawk Golf Course development; says the city is growing too fast and should slow down.
growth.quote: We're growing too quickly. We can't go back, but I think we can slow down some of the growth

budget.stance: Blames the city's deficit partly on state tax abatements for incoming companies; favors reducing abatements, reforming property tax, and seeking state revenue help.
budget.quote: They're running out a deficit now...part of that I blame on all the tax abatements that the governor has given for companies to move here.

taxes.stance: Wants to reduce state tax abatements given to companies relocating to the area and reform the property-tax structure.

candidates.name: Wendi Rawson
candidates.bio: Small-business owner and founder of Engine 8 Urban Winery (2019); born and raised in Fallon and a Reed High School graduate, running as a fresh-perspective outsider.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates: Sparks Mayor
KOLO: Mayoral candidates file amid fiscal concerns

budget.stance: Says the city has lost its way and attributes the deficit to current leadership; opposes new taxes on residents and wants to capture funds owed to the city.
budget.quote: Taxing our citizens is not an option. It's time to get in there and fine-tune where we are

growth.stance: Criticizes the mayor's six-year focus on the federal lands bill as neglect of in-city issues; favors small-business support and downtown revitalization.
growth.quote: Lands Bill is his focus...he's missed the mark on what's going on within city limits.

candidates.name: Jamie O'Connell
candidates.bio: Financial and billing specialist/analyst and Sparks High School graduate (age 36); registered Democrat focused on transportation, public safety, and homelessness.

sources.label: RGJ/AOL: Election 2026 Sparks City Council Ward 2

budget.stance: Would never cut first responders but is open to cutting redundant consulting contracts or departments.
budget.quote: I would never cut first responders

publicSafety.stance: Opposes the proposed first-responder fee, arguing residents already face inflation and fees might deter people from calling for emergencies.

growth.stance: Supports growth in smart locations near existing roads with developer-funded improvements and early, transparent community input.

candidates.name: Hunter W. Rand
candidates.bio: Director/producer, broadcast engineer, and former teacher/university faculty (age 30) with a UNR journalism degree; member of the United States Pirate Party, focused on cost of living, infrastructure-first growth, and transparency.

sources.label: RGJ/AOL: Election 2026 Sparks City Council Ward 2
Ballotpedia: Hunter W. Rand

budget.stance: Would protect special-events funding as economic driver but would consider cutting surveillance-tech grants (ShotSpotter, Flock Safety) that lack measurable results.

publicSafety.stance: Supports a first-responder fee only with strict guardrails: no balance billing for residents and compassionate provisions for students and visitors.

growth.stance: Infrastructure-first: roads, traffic mitigation, and public safety must precede new development.
growth.quote: Roads, traffic mitigation and public safety must come first.

candidates.name: Dian VanderWell
candidates.bio: Sparks City Council member for Ward 2 since September 2020 (appointed, then elected 2022); licensed mortgage and real estate agent (age 60) with TMCC/UNR business background.

sources.label: RGJ/AOL: Election 2026 Sparks City Council Ward 2

budget.stance: Would never cut frontline public safety; would instead examine lower-impact programs like festivals or consultant projects.
budget.quote: I would never cut funding that directly supports public safety...especially frontline police, fire, and emergency response.

publicSafety.stance: Cautiously supportive of a first-responder fee focused on insurance and non-residents, insisting on no surprise bills and protections so it never deters 911 calls.

growth.stance: Favors smarter growth over sprawl: infill and mixed-use housing near jobs to reduce traffic; supports addressing Nevada's property-tax system.
growth.quote: smarter growth, not sprawl: infill and mixed-use housing near jobs

candidates.name: Anthony J. Goulet
candidates.bio: Candidate for Sparks City Council Ward 2; did not respond to media questionnaires and reported no campaign funds raised.

sources.label: RGJ/AOL: Election 2026 Sparks City Council Ward 2

candidates.name: Damon Harrell
candidates.bio: Real estate agent, Panasonic employee, and U.S. Marine Corps veteran (age 54) running for office for the first time.

sources.label: RGJ/AOL: See who's running for Sparks City Council Ward 4

budget.stance: Would never cut public-safety funding, calling police and fire core services.
budget.quote: I would never cut funding for public safety — police and fire are core services.

growth.stance: Opposes reckless development; says infrastructure must precede growth to protect existing residents.

publicSafety.stance: Supports charging repeat commercial users a first-responder fee but opposes residential charges; prioritizes emergency response over billing.

candidates.name: Adam Mayberry
candidates.bio: Communications professional and part-time college instructor (age 55) currently serving on the Washoe County School Board (District F), which he is leaving to run for council; holds degrees from Embry-Riddle and UNR.

sources.label: RGJ/AOL: See who's running for Sparks City Council Ward 4
Ballotpedia: Tracey Thomas (Ward 4 race page)

publicSafety.stance: Stresses fast, professional emergency response; prefers exploring federal Medicaid supplemental reimbursement over a first-responder fee.
publicSafety.quote: When someone calls 911, they deserve a fast, professional response.

budget.stance: Supports cutting consulting and administrative overhead rather than frontline services.

growth.stance: Emphasizes infrastructure readiness before approving new development.

candidates.name: Sean McCaffrey
candidates.bio: Owner of Bin Masters of Northern Nevada (age 53), running as a first-time candidate.

sources.label: RGJ/AOL: See who's running for Sparks City Council Ward 4

publicSafety.stance: Fiercely opposed to a first-responder fee, warning it could deter fixed-income residents from calling 911; prioritizes reopening Fire Station 5 at the Golden Eagle location.

growth.stance: Calls for stronger regional coordination and criticizes 'rubberstamped' projects lacking vision.

budget.stance: Notes current funds are earmarked; supports fire consolidation and revenue diversification.

candidates.name: Tracey Thomas
candidates.bio: Retired (age 62) with prior work in mortgage lending, appraisal, and county technology; running for office for the first time.

sources.label: RGJ/AOL: See who's running for Sparks City Council Ward 4
Ballotpedia: Tracey Thomas

growth.stance: Prioritizes maintaining existing assets over new expansion; supports higher-density town centers over sprawl and eastward development aligned with I-80 widening.

budget.stance: Emphasizes sustainability over growth during lean years.

publicSafety.stance: Calls the 'first-responder fee' name misleading and wants alternatives explored before any implementation.

candidates.name: Sheena Harvey
candidates.bio: Educator with a master's degree and doctorate in education; UNR graduate and longtime Reno resident active on community and government boards.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates: WCSD School Board District F

education.stance: Identifies chronic absenteeism as the most pressing issue; wants after-school programs, intramural sports, and extracurriculars to boost student engagement.
education.quote: programs and services for our students to be in school after school and you know intramural sports or extracurriculars

candidates.name: Brooke Westlake
candidates.bio: 37-year Reno resident, WCSD graduate, and parent advocate with two sons (one in public school, one in charter school).

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates: WCSD School Board District F

education.stance: Cites budget deficits, low enrollment, and chronic absenteeism as major concerns and says fixing them requires uncomfortable conversations about revising existing policies.
education.quote: budget deficits, the low enrollment rates, and then the chronic absenteeism

budget.stance: Names district budget deficits and declining enrollment as top concerns requiring difficult policy review.

candidates.name: Scott Benton
candidates.bio: Candidate for Washoe County School Board Trustee District F; was unavailable for the candidate interview series and limited public information is available.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates: WCSD School Board District F
